name: review-work
tagline: 5-agent parallel PR review orchestrator (goal, QA execution, code quality, security, context mining) gating implementation handoff.
verdict: No malicious behavior found: read-only git/file analysis, no secret harvesting, no destructive commands, no hidden instructions. Main risk: it's built for the oh-my-openagent/Codex harness (oracle/unspecified-high agent types, multi_agent_v1 calls) plain Claude Code lacks, so it may not run as-is. Also 605 lines, over the ~500-line budget.

highlights.summary: Agent 5 (Context Mining, lines 448-531) is told to pull content from Slack/Notion/Discord/GitHub issues and PR comments and fold 'discovered_context' straight into the review verdict, with no instruct
No stated negative triggers — doesn't say to skip this for tiny/trivial diffs or early draft work where 5 parallel agents would be overkill.
Body is written in OpenCode-native call syntax (task(subagent_type="oracle", ...), lines 129-196 etc.) while a bolted-on preamble (lines 5-58) tells the Codex harness to mentally translate every call
